name: New Zealand Kiwifruit and Yogurt Bowl

ingredients.ingredient: Chobani Plain Low-Fat Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Slice the kiwifruit.
Spoon the yogurt into a bowl and top with the kiwifruit.
Drizzle with honey.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chobani Plain Low-Fat Greek Yogurt, Plain
Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Toast the oats in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until fragrant and lightly golden, then let cool.
Slice the kiwifruit into rounds.
Spoon the yogurt into a bowl, arrange the kiwifruit over the top, and scatter with the toasted oats.
Drizzle generously with honey - New Zealand's Zespri kiwifruit is a major export and breakfast staple.
description: Kiwifruit, despite the name association with the bird, is actually native to China - New Zealand pioneered its commercial cultivation and export, which is how the fruit got its common name.

name: New Zealand Bacon and Egg Pie
steps: Fry the bacon until just crisp.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, and cold water, and line a small dish.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scattered bacon, and bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until set and golden.
steps_detailed: Fry the chopped bacon in a dry pan until just crisp, then let cool slightly.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, a pinch of salt, and cold water. Roll out and line a small pie dish.
Whisk the eggs with the milk and black pepper, then pour into the pastry shell and scatter the bacon over the top.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until the filling is set and the pastry is golden.
A classic New Zealand picnic and lunchbox staple, usually eaten cold or at room temperature.
description: Bacon and egg pie, a simple savory pie, a beloved New Zealand picnic and lunch staple.

name: New Zealand Weet-Bix Style Breakfast

ingredients.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Combine the cereal, milk, and sugar in a bowl.
Let sit 2-3 minutes to soften slightly before eating.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Combine the cereal and milk in a bowl and let sit 2-3 minutes so the cereal softens slightly, the traditional way wheat biscuit cereal is eaten.
Sprinkle with the brown sugar and top with sliced kiwifruit.
A simple, iconic Kiwi breakfast.
description: Weet-Bix, a wheat biscuit cereal, is a New Zealand and Australian breakfast icon.

name: New Zealand Meat Pie
steps: Brown the beef and simmer with the broth until thickened, about 10 minutes.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, and cold water.
Fill the pastry, top with more pastry, and bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Brown the ground beef with the onion, then add the beef broth and simmer 8-10 minutes until thick and gravy-like. Let cool.
Make a simple pastry from the flour, butter, a pinch of salt, and cold water, rolling into two rounds.
Line a small pie dish with one round, fill with the cooled meat mixture, and top with the second round, crimping the edges and cutting a vent.
Brush with the egg wash and bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until deeply golden - New Zealanders eat more meat pies per capita than almost anywhere else.
description: The meat pie is considered close to New Zealand's national dish, eaten constantly from bakeries, service stations, and stadiums alike.

name: New Zealand Whitebait Fritters

ingredients.note: finely chopped, as a whitebait stand-in

steps: Mix the finely chopped fish with the beaten eggs and flour into a loose batter.
Fry spoonfuls in the butter 2-3 minutes per side until golden.
Serve with a squeeze of lemon.

ingredients_full.note: finely chopped, as a whitebait stand-in

steps_detailed: Heat the butter in a skillet over medium heat and fry spoonfuls of the batter, flattened slightly, 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Serve with a good squeeze of lime, echoing the classic lemon wedge - whitebait fritters are a treasured seasonal delicacy on the West Coast of the South Island.
description: Finely chop the fish (traditionally tiny whole whitebait fish, which cook whole into the fritter) and mix with the beaten eggs and flour into a loose, cohesive batter.

name: New Zealand Pumpkin Soup

ingredients.note: peeled, chunked, as a pumpkin stand-in

steps: Saute the onion until soft, then add the sweet potato and broth and simmer 15-18 minutes until tender.
Blend until smooth.
Serve with a swirl of sour cream.

ingredients_full.note: peeled, chunked, as a pumpkin stand-in

steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic for 4-5 minutes until soft.
Add the sweet potato (standing in for New Zealand's beloved pumpkin), broth, and cinnamon, and simmer 18-20 minutes until completely tender.
Blend until smooth and creamy, thinning with a little water if needed.
description: Serve hot with a swirl of sour cream - pumpkin soup is a beloved winter staple across New Zealand.

name: New Zealand Green-Lipped Mussels
steps: Steam the mussels in the wine and garlic until they open, about 5-6 minutes.
Discard any that stay closed.
Toss with the butter and serve with the broth.
steps_detailed: Saute the garlic briefly in half the butter, then add the wine and bring to a simmer.
Add the mussels, cover, and steam 5-7 minutes until they all open - discard any that remain closed.
Toss with the remaining butter, cilantro, and lime juice.
Serve hot with the flavorful broth - New Zealand's green-lipped mussels are a prized export, farmed extensively in the Marlborough Sounds.
description: Green-lipped mussels are native specifically to New Zealand waters, prized for their size and flavor.

name: New Zealand Hangi-Style Lamb and Vegetables

ingredients.note: formed into a small roast, or use lamb chops

steps: Season the lamb well and roast at 325F, covered, for 1-1.5 hours until very tender.
Steam or simmer the sweet potato, cabbage, and carrot until tender.
Serve together - traditionally cooked in an underground earth oven, this stovetop version keeps the same slow-cooked, tender character.

ingredients_full.note: formed into a small roast, or use lamb chops

steps_detailed: Season the lamb generously with garlic, oregano, and a little oil, and shape into a compact roast.
Roast in a covered dish at 325F (165C) for 1.25-1.5 hours until completely tender, basting occasionally with the pan juices.
Steam or simmer the sweet potato, cabbage, and carrot separately until tender, about 15-18 minutes.
description: Serve everything together - a hangi is traditionally cooked in a pit with heated stones, a Maori cooking method for large gatherings; this recaptures the same slow-cooked, smoky-tender result at home.

name: New Zealand Fish and Chips
steps: Cut the potato into fries and fry until golden, about 10-12 minutes, then set aside.
Dust the fish in flour and fry until golden and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve together, traditionally wrapped in paper.
steps_detailed: Cut the potatoes into fries and fry in the oil over medium-high heat until golden and crisp, 10-14 minutes total, then set aside.
Whisk the flour, baking powder, and a splash of cold water into a thick, smooth batter.
Dip the fish in the batter and fry in the same oil, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through.
Serve with a squeeze of lime - a Friday night tradition across New Zealand, often enjoyed straight from the paper by the beach.
description: Fish and chips, brought by British settlers, remains one of New Zealand's most common takeaway meals.

name: New Zealand Lamb Shanks
steps: Brown the lamb, then add the onion, carrot, and tomatoes with a splash of water.
Cover and simmer 1-1.5 hours until the lamb is completely tender.
Serve with mashed potato.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb well, then remove and set aside.
Saute the onion, carrot, and garlic in the same pot for 5-6 minutes until softened.
Return the lamb, add the diced tomatoes, beef broth, bay leaf, and oregano, and bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook over low heat 1.25-1.5 hours until the lamb is completely tender and falling apart.
description: Remove the bay leaf and serve with mashed potato - New Zealand lamb is famous worldwide, and slow-braised shanks are a beloved comfort dish.

name: New Zealand Boil Up
steps: Simmer the pork in water 30-35 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the potato and cabbage, and simmer 15 minutes more.
Mix the flour with a little water into a soft dough, drop spoonfuls into the simmering pot, and cook 10 minutes until the doughboys are cooked through.
steps_detailed: Simmer the pork with the onion and black pepper in 3 cups water for 30-35 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the potato and simmer 12-15 minutes, then add the cabbage and simmer 8-10 more minutes.
Mix the flour and baking powder with a pinch of salt and just enough water to form a soft dough for the doughboys (dumplings).
Drop spoonfuls of the dough into the simmering pot and cook, covered, 10-12 minutes until they're cooked through and fluffy.
description: A cherished Maori comfort food, traditionally made with pork bones and watercress, passed down through generations.

name: New Zealand Cheese Rolls
steps: Mix the cheese with the milk into a spreadable paste.
Spread onto the bread, roll up tightly, and toast in the buttered pan until golden on all sides.
steps_detailed: Mix the shredded cheese with the milk and grated onion (if using) into a soft, spreadable paste.
Spread generously onto flattened, crust-removed bread slices and roll up tightly into a cylinder.
Toast the rolls in the buttered pan over medium heat, turning to brown all sides, 5-6 minutes total until golden and the cheese has melted inside.
A beloved Southland/Otago regional specialty, especially popular at school galas and community events.
description: Cheese rolls, bread rolled around a cheesy filling and grilled, are a genuine South Island New Zealand specialty.

name: New Zealand Sausage Sizzle
steps: Season and grill or pan-fry the pork until well browned and cooked through.
Saute the onion until soft and golden.
Serve the sausage on the bread, topped with the onion and a swipe of tomato sauce.
steps_detailed: Season the pork generously with garlic and pepper, mimicking a classic sausage flavor, and shape if needed.
Grill or pan-fry over medium-high heat until well browned outside and cooked through, 5-6 minutes per side.
Saute the onion in the oil until soft and golden, 6-8 minutes.

Serve the sausage on a folded slice of white bread, topped with the onions and a swipe of tomato sauce - the classic 'sausage sizzle' fundraiser staple found outside every hardware store and polling booth.
description: The sausage sizzle, a sausage on a slice of bread from a barbecue, is a New Zealand fundraising and community-event tradition.

name: New Zealand Pineapple Lumps Style Bites

ingredients.ingredient: Pineapple, canned, juice pack, solids and liquids

steps: Pat the chopped pineapple very dry.
Melt the cocoa powder with the sugar and a splash of water into a thick chocolate-like glaze.
Coat the pineapple pieces in the glaze and chill until set.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Pineapple, canned, juice pack, solids and liquids

steps_detailed: Pat the chopped pineapple pieces very dry with paper towels - excess moisture will keep the coating from setting.
Melt the butter with the cocoa powder, sugar, and a small splash of water over low heat into a thick, glossy chocolate glaze.
Toss the pineapple pieces in the glaze to coat evenly.
Chill on a lined tray until set, about 20-30 minutes - inspired by the iconic Kiwi lolly (candy) that pairs chocolate and pineapple flavor.
description: Pineapple Lumps, a chocolate-coated chewy pineapple candy, is a distinctly New Zealand confectionery icon.

name: New Zealand Pavlova

ingredients.note: tbsp, whipped, as a cream stand-in

steps: Whip the egg whites until foamy, then gradually add the sugar and whip until thick, glossy, and holding stiff peaks.
Spread into a round on a lined baking sheet and bake at 250F for 50-60 minutes until crisp outside, then turn off the oven and let cool inside.
Top with the whipped cream and sliced kiwifruit.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, whipped with a little sugar

steps_detailed: Whip the egg whites until foamy, then gradually add the sugar a spoonful at a time, whipping until thick, glossy, and holding stiff, shiny peaks.
Gently fold in the cornstarch and vinegar for the classic marshmallowy center.
Spread into a round mound on a parchment-lined sheet, smoothing the sides.
Bake at 250F (120C) for 55-65 minutes until crisp outside, then turn off the oven and let it cool completely inside with the door ajar.
description: Top with whipped cream and sliced kiwifruit just before serving - New Zealand and Australia have argued for decades over who invented this, but kiwifruit on top is a distinctly Kiwi touch.

name: New Zealand Afghan Biscuits
steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then mix in the flour and cocoa powder.
Fold in the crushed corn flakes and shape into small rounds.
Bake at 350F for 12-15 minutes until set.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar together until light, then beat in the vanilla.
Sift in the flour and cocoa powder and mix until just combined into a soft dough.
Gently fold in the crushed corn flakes, keeping some texture rather than overmixing.
Shape into small rounds, flatten slightly, and place on a baking sheet.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 12-16 minutes until just set - a uniquely New Zealand chocolate biscuit named, somewhat mysteriously, after Afghanistan.
description: A uniquely New Zealand chocolate biscuit, named somewhat mysteriously after Afghanistan - crushed corn flakes are the real, distinctive ingredient that gives it its characteristic crunchy texture.

name: New Zealand Louise Cake
steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then work in the flour to form a shortbread base and press into a small pan.
Spread the jam over the base.
Whip the egg white with a little sugar and coconut, spread over the jam, and bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar, then work in the flour until a soft shortbread dough forms. Press evenly into a small buttered pan.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 10-12 minutes until just set, then remove and spread the jam evenly over the warm base.
Whip the egg whites until foamy, then gradually add the sugar for the topping and whip until glossy, and fold in the shredded coconut.
Spread the coconut meringue over the jam layer.
Return to the oven and bake 15-18 more minutes until the topping is golden.
Cool completely before slicing - a classic New Zealand school-bake-sale favorite, layering shortbread, jam, and coconut meringue.
description: Louise cake, a raspberry jam and coconut meringue slice, a beloved New Zealand baking classic.

name: New Zealand Kiwifruit Salsa
steps: Combine the diced kiwifruit, onion, lime juice, and jalapeno.
Season with salt and let sit 10 minutes.
Serve alongside grilled fish or pork.
steps_detailed: Combine the diced kiwifruit, onion, jalapeno, and cilantro in a bowl.
Stir in the lime juice and season with salt.
Let sit 10-15 minutes so the flavors meld, then serve alongside grilled fish, pork, or chicken.
description: A simple salsa built on kiwifruit, which New Zealand pioneered growing commercially.

name: New Zealand Mint Sauce
steps: Combine the chopped mint, vinegar, and sugar.
Let sit 10 minutes before serving alongside roast lamb.
steps_detailed: Finely chop the mint and combine with the vinegar, sugar, and oil in a small bowl.
Stir well and let sit at least 10-15 minutes so the sugar dissolves and the mint infuses the vinegar.
The classic accompaniment to roast lamb across New Zealand and the wider Commonwealth.
description: Mint sauce, a sharp vinegar-mint condiment, a classic pairing for roast lamb, reflecting New Zealand's strong sheep-farming tradition.

name: New Zealand Honey Glaze
steps: Whisk the honey, mustard, and lime juice together until smooth.
Brush over roasted chicken, pork, or vegetables in the last few minutes of cooking.
steps_detailed: Whisk the honey, mustard, lime juice, and black pepper together until smooth and well combined.
Brush generously over roasted chicken, pork, or root vegetables in the last 10-15 minutes of cooking so it caramelizes without burning.
New Zealand honey, especially manuka, is a prized export known for its distinctive character.
description: A simple honey glaze, manuka honey being a distinctly New Zealand specialty.
